title: Post-excision wound review
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

treatment_and_management.text: Supports clinician-documented postoperative wound-care information, infection-warning discussion, and who to contact if concern develops.
treatment_and_management.rationale: The exact postoperative information section supports recording only a clinician-entered plan, safety net, arranged follow-up and patient questions.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-documented postoperative wound-care information, infection-warning discussion, and who to contact if concern develops.

source.title: Surgical site infections: prevention and treatment — Recommendations

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.4 — information, wound care, and infection recognition

follow_up.text: Supports review of suspected infection, cellulitis, microbiology context, structured wound care, and local heat, redness, pain, swelling, discharge, separation, abscess, fever, or systemic signs.
follow_up.rationale: The exact postoperative-wound section supports documenting excision-wound interval history, change, pain or symptoms, clinician skin inspection, infection signs, general appearance and available pathology or microbiology results without diagnosing infection or dehiscence.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports review of suspected infection, cellulitis, microbiology context, structured wound care, and local heat, redness, pain, swelling, discharge, separation, abscess, fever, or systemic signs.

source_limitations: Excision type, date and site, closure, bleeding, wound dimensions, pathology details, culture, diagnosis, dressing, cleansing, medicine, suture plan, referral, and timing remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
